results.name: Keelvar
results.topBullish: Burry is right. Memory is cyclical. Chips will reprice. The $500B Korean supply expansion IS the beginning of the end for chip margins. What retail is missing: Cheaper hardware = more clusters needed = more power demand. @captainkeel $KEEL owns the power.
results.topBearish: $KEEL suffers core small-cap infrastructure headwinds: slow project award cadence, thin recurring revenue, and heavy field operation costs that eat into margins. With almost no institutional long-term holders, every price surge only lasts as long as retail social media hype.
results.summary: The post sample is overwhelmingly dominated by spam — repeated WhatsApp group invite bots and generic promotional content account for well over 40% of all posts, triggering spam detection and zeroing the sentiment score. The small number of genuine financial posts reflect a mixed picture: bullish positioning around data center power demand and Moses Lake negotiations, offset by bearish concern over price decline from $7.37 to $4.59 and weak fundamental metrics.
